Title: Gene editing of three BnITPK genes in tetraploid oilseed rape leads to significant reduction of phytic acid in seeds

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phosphorus storage compound in seeds; anti-nutritional factor reducing mineral bioavailability in monogastric animals and contributing to phosphorus pollution/eutrophication when undigested
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates lower inositol phosphates in the phytic acid pathway, contributing to conversion of inositol tris/tetrakisphosphates toward higher phosphorylated intermediates required for phytic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: higher phosphorylated inositol phosphates leading toward phytic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediates required for phytic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in this study; knockout contributes additively but alone is insufficient for significant reduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function contributes to reduced seed phytic acid and increased free inorganic phosphorus when combined with Bn.ITPK1.C09 and Bn.ITPK4.C04b mutations; double BnITPK1 mutants alone did not significantly reduce phytic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bn.ITPK1.A10 was one of the three functional BnITPK paralogs targeted by CRISPR-Cas9 in cv. Haydn. 2. Homozygous triple mutants affecting Bn.ITPK1.A10, Bn.ITPK1.C09 and Bn.ITPK4.C04b showed significant seed phytic acid reduction (27.2%–35.3%) and increased inorganic phosphorus. 3. Double mutants affecting BnITPK1 paralogs alone were not sufficient for significant PA reduction, indicating additive paralog effects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seeds; selected as a functional paralog relevant to seed phytic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; paralog-specific genotyping and Sanger sequencing; segregation to homozygous non-transgenic mutants; seed phytic acid HPLC quantification; inorganic phosphorus assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse long-day conditions (16 h light/8 h dark) at 22°C; plants not fertilized to avoid phosphorus contamination
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana U6-26 promoter (sgRNA, constitutive); parsley Ubi4-2 promoter (Cas9, constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression data from published transcript datasets cited for developing seeds and leaves (Shah et al., 2018); no accession reported in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious negative effects on growth, seedling vigor, or seed oil content in mutant combinations analyzed under controlled conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for low-phytate rapeseed breeding, but effective deployment requires multiplex editing of multiple paralogs due to gene redundancy in polyploid B. napus
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One SNP-discriminating family members with low expression were avoided; no off-targets detected in BLAST analysis against the rapeseed genome

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phosphorus storage compound in seeds; anti-nutritional factor reducing mineral bioavailability in monogastric animals and contributing to phosphorus pollution/eutrophication when undigested
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates lower inositol phosphates in the phytic acid pathway, contributing to production of higher phosphorylated inositol phosphates preceding phytic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: higher phosphorylated inositol phosphates leading toward phytic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediates required for phytic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in this study; acts additively with other paralogs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation contributes to significant reduction of seed phytic acid and increase of inorganic phosphorus only in triple mutant background; a 6-bp deletion allele in this gene was associated with the lowest PA line
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bn.ITPK1.C09 was a core CRISPR target among the functional BnITPK paralogs. 2. Triple mutants carrying a Bn.ITPK1.C09 edited allele together with mutations in Bn.ITPK1.A10 and Bn.ITPK4.C04b showed significant PA reduction and higher free Pi. 3. The line with a 6-bp deletion in Bn.ITPK1.C09 had the lowest PA content, consistent with disruption near an ATP-binding site.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seeds; considered an essential functional paralog for PA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; locus-specific sequencing; inheritance analysis; homozygous mutant selection; HPLC measurement of phytic acid; Pi colorimetric assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse long-day conditions (16 h light/8 h dark) at 22°C; no phosphorus fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana U6-26 promoter (sgRNA, constitutive); parsley Ubi4-2 promoter (Cas9, constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression data from published transcript datasets cited for developing seeds and leaves (Shah et al., 2018); no accession reported in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable penalties in seedling vigor, plant growth, or oil content in analyzed mutants under controlled conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for multiplex editing stacks to reduce phytate in rapeseed meal while maintaining oil content and agronomic performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein modeling suggested that the 6-bp deletion causes loss of two amino acids within an ATP-binding site

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major phosphorus storage compound in seeds; anti-nutritional factor reducing mineral absorption and contributing to environmental phosphorus loading
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates inositol phosphate intermediates in the phytic acid biosynthetic pathway, supporting production of higher inositol polyphosphates required for phytate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: higher phosphorylated inositol phosphates leading toward phytic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediates required for phytic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key paralog required in combination with BnITPK1 paralogs to achieve significant phytate reduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with Bn.ITPK1.A10 and Bn.ITPK1.C09 mutations for significant reduction of seed phytic acid and elevation of inorganic phosphorus; absence of BnITPK4 mutation in double mutants corresponded to no significant PA reduction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bn.ITPK4.C04b was one of the three essential BnITPK genes edited in this study. 2. Significant PA reduction occurred only when this BnITPK4 paralog was mutated together with two BnITPK1 paralogs. 3. The results demonstrate functional redundancy and additive action of BnITPK paralogs in tetraploid rapeseed.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functional paralog selected for mutagenesis; lowly expressed SNP-containing family members were deprioritized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; paralog-specific sequencing; segregation analysis; selection of homozygous triple mutants; HPLC quantification of phytic acid; inorganic phosphorus measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse long-day conditions (16 h light/8 h dark) at 22°C; no phosphorus fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabidopsis thaliana U6-26 promoter (sgRNA, constitutive); parsley Ubi4-2 promoter (Cas9, constitutive)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression data from published transcript datasets cited for developing seeds and leaves (Shah et al., 2018); no accession reported in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious agronomic or oil-content penalty detected in mutant lines analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important component of multiplex editing strategy for generating low-phytate rapeseed; should be combined with BnITPK1 paralog edits due to polyploid redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bn.ITPK4.A05 was also targeted initially but its mutations were not inherited in one key lineage; Bn.ITPK4.C04a was excluded because it could not be amplified from multiple genotypes
